meetings.title: Fiscal Meeting
meetings.notes: Reviewed the variance. We spent decently but still have a big variance.

meetings.title: Caravan lab meeting
meetings.attendees: Jen Moslemi, Jon Bridgman, Katie Love, Chase Nuuhiwa, Ian McCabe
meetings.notes:
Noted the pacing was off and needs a better setup for why we are watching this video.
Suggested moving the key line on importance, better music, and animations.
Caravan will come back with a new edit by the end of the month with better pacing, music, and something at the beginning. Decide then what a contract extension would look like in terms of funds and timing.

meetings.title: NEP 40th meeting
meetings.notes:
EPA headquarters is working on a 40th anniversary campaign called Eat, Work, Play.
NEP folks were not impressed.
Politically, we should support EPA staff as they sell the NEP program to upper management.
EPA team is bringing the campaign to upper management on July 1.
Discussed survey results and Codex summary at www.psp.wa.gov/index-NEP.php.
NEP Tech Transfer is in October. Check that funds were added to attend and alert leadership that there is a communications network meeting at the transfer.

meetings.title: Commitments Dashboard
meetings.attendees: Don, Alex, Wess, Jen Burke, Robb, Ashley, Jon
meetings.notes:
Looked at how commitments are showing up in the Action Agenda Explorer.
Discussed a separate commitments dashboard.
Suggested using the creative brief template to clarify the audience and need for the separate dashboard.
Discussed both an internal analysis tool and a public tool.
Open question: How should commitments show up in the State of the Sound?

meetings.title: Ian One on One
meetings.notes:
Give Ian the tugboat presser text to prepare in GovDelivery.
Discuss special issue website posting and social media support.
Discuss economic data report for website placement and social media support.
Add task to clean up the news boxes on the homepage with fresh content.
Social Science article intro will go on the Human Wellbeing pages and in a news box on the homepage.
Katie is working on a new Human Wellbeing draft, but it is not clear whether it replaces existing pages. Need clarity from Becky.
Ian proposed a three-act structure for the Salmon in Schools video: fun and joy raising little salmon; poignant serious section about what salmon mean to kids; resolution of letting the salmon go.

meetings.title: Action Agenda 1 pager
meetings.attendees: Don, Robb, Doug, Katie, Chase, Ian, Jon
meetings.notes:
Don feels a simple one pager will work for him and Signe. If something more detailed is needed, use the executive summary.
Signe will take the draft and write something that works for the Unconference by next Tuesday.
Aim to have a final draft by the end of the week.
Send to Chase on June 29 so he can design a draft before vacation on July 2.
Final deadline needs to be set before the July 24 Unconference to allow Spanish translation and printing.
To do: send a final schedule detailing and refining these steps.

meetings.title: Action Agenda Web Update
meetings.notes:
Sent links to the fact sheet, rollout plan, current web content, and press release.
Jon owes the team a schedule for getting the web content and fact sheet complete.
Agreed it is better to start now.
Final deadline is whenever EPA approves.
Emphasize the Explorer in the website and fact sheet.
Fall workshops are for insiders and do not need wide promotion.
Discussed getting a long list of quotes for the press release. Robb and Doug will generate a list of potential quotable people.

meetings.title: Riparian Roundtable
meetings.attendees: Katie Love, Jen Grimm, Jon Bridgman
meetings.notes:
There will be two fact sheets that need designing and a report that needs a cover.
Jen will hand off the documents by the end of the month.
Content deadline was recorded as July 2X. After that, hand off to Chase, who will have one week for design.
Jen is telling Larry there will be something for him to look at by the end of July.

meetings.title: State of the Sound Road Trip
meetings.notes:
Scott liked what was created last time.
He supported creating narratives around themes Mindy chooses for political reasons, while keeping State of the Sound as a fact-based comprehensive overview.
Suggested not publishing the narratives in the actual State of the Sound so they do not get caught in the approval process. They could be used for presentations afterward.
Earlier data deadline could reduce the summer crunch.
Scott asked whether the Science Panel letter would need to be submitted earlier. Jon suggested letters would still be written close to the end.

meetings.title: Katie One on One
meetings.notes:
Fixed Katie's GovDelivery issue, but it highlighted concern that GovDelivery is not allowing signups.
Talked about whether the GovDelivery press release list is current.
